subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to retrieve Datadog license allocation data including license tier, seat count, allocation date, and any modifications or changes over time
Query SQL warehouse to retrieve actual Datadog usage metrics including daily active users, feature usage by type, API call volumes, and metric ingestion rates over the same time period

Reconcile license allocation data with actual usage metrics to identify discrepancies, calculate utilization rates, and analyze growth patterns including trend direction, velocity, and forecasting needs

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks are necessary: (1) retrieve_license_allocation and (2) retrieve_usage_metrics are separate sql_gen calls because they query distinct data domains (licensing vs. operational metrics) with different schemas and aggregation logic, and both are pre-requisites for reconciliation. Assigned mid-tier for each SQL query due to multi-table joins needed to capture allocation history and usage dimensions. (3) reconcile_and_analyze_trends is a frontier-tier cross_recon task that combines both datasets, computes utilization gaps, identifies growth patterns, and generates trend analysis and recommendations — this requires complex multi-signal reasoning to surface actionable insights from the reconciliation. Frontier tier justifies the complexity of comparative analysis and forecasting logic needed to answer the "trends" and "growth patterns" aspects of the query.

output.sql: SELECT cf.license_id, cf.product, cf.event_type, cf.event_date, cf.daily_active_seats, cf.amount_usd, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, LAG(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id ORDER BY cf.event_date) AS prev_seats, cf.daily_active_seats - LAG(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id ORDER BY cf.event_date) AS seat_change, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter) AS quarterly_total_seats, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter) AS quarterly_avg_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter) AS quarterly_spend, COUNT(cf.event_id) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter) AS quarterly_event_count, MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id ORDER BY cf.event_date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_30d_max_seats, MIN(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id ORDER BY cf.event_date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_30d_min_seats, AVG(cf.amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.license_id ORDER BY cf.event_date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_30d_avg_spend FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%datadog%' OR cf.product ILIKE '%datadog%' ORDER BY cf.license_id, cf.event_date

SELECT cf.product, cf.event_type, cf.event_date, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_users, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS distinct_users, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MIN(cf.event_date) AS period_start, MAX(cf.event_date) AS period_end FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Datadog' AND cf.event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY cf.product, cf.event_type, cf.event_date ORDER BY cf.event_date ASC, cf.product, cf.event_type
